entries.description: Rolling 24-bar 5-minute close-return volatility.
entries.hypothesis: High short-horizon realized volatility should reduce long-only selection quality after costs and execution constraints.

entries.description: Rolling 12-bar 5-minute close-return volatility.
entries.hypothesis: A medium intraday volatility penalty may complement the 24-bar risk filter with less regime lag.

entries.description: Rolling 6-bar 5-minute close-return volatility.
entries.hypothesis: A short intraday volatility penalty may catch fast deterioration before longer windows react.

entries.description: Absolute close-to-close return scaled by turnover.
entries.hypothesis: Price impact can capture intraday crowding or liquidity stress that is not captured by raw volume or turnover ratios.

entries.description: Current turnover divided by the 48-bar rolling average minus one.
entries.hypothesis: Persistent turnover expansion can indicate participation and tradability, but must be evaluated against cost and crowding.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 2026-05-20 legacy revalidation kept the factor on watchlist. Full and high-cost returns were positive, but admission remained watchlist and the factor belongs in horizon or policy review rather than the confirmed core.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retest only with stricter turnover controls or entry/exit buffers, and require positive incremental contribution after correlation and contribution caps.

entries.description: Current turnover standardized by a 48-bar rolling mean and standard deviation.
entries.hypothesis: Extreme turnover shocks may be informative, but the latest admission report marks the standalone signal as economically weak after costs.

research_memory.negative_findings: Standard admission kept the factor on watchlist because standalone economics were weak after transaction-cost and stability gates; do not reuse as a primary alpha without a targeted redesign.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a conditional crowding or liquidity-stress overlay, or after demonstrating positive portfolio contribution net of costs in annual slices.

entries.description: Current volume divided by the 48-bar rolling average minus one.
entries.hypothesis: Volume expansion may help separate tradable attention from volatility-only effects, but current standalone economics are weak.

research_memory.negative_findings: Volume expansion was statistically usable but did not clear the economic or stability bar in standard admission; raw volume ratio should not be reintroduced as a standalone long signal.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with price-confirmation, liquidity-adjusted transforms, or a regime filter that proves incremental portfolio contribution after costs.

entries.description: Twelve-bar close-to-close return, currently used in inverted direction when reviewed.
entries.hypothesis: Short intraday continuation may reverse after execution lag and costs; keep as a controlled watchlist signal before reuse.

research_memory.negative_findings: Short intraday momentum required inverted treatment and remained on watchlist because execution lag and annual stability were not strong enough for default promotion.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a reversal or overreaction variant with explicit holding-period and cost controls, not as raw short-horizon continuation.

entries.description: Close price position within the rolling 48-bar intraday high-low range, centered around zero.
entries.hypothesis: A close near the top of the recent range may indicate stronger short-horizon demand than a close near the bottom of the range.

research_memory.negative_findings: Rejected in the 2026-05-15 batch: rank IC was positive but directional hit rate and stable-year count were insufficient, so range position did not show reliable standalone selection value.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only after redesigning as an overextension reversal or breakout-confirmation feature with materially different transform and documented stability improvement.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar high-low range volatility based on log intrabar range.
entries.hypothesis: High high-low range volatility should signal unstable execution conditions and lower long-only selection quality after costs.

entries.description: Directional close-to-close move divided by the total absolute 1-bar path length over the same window.
entries.hypothesis: Very efficient intraday directional moves may be overextended after execution lag; the current admission report supports the inverted direction.

entries.description: Close-to-VWAP stretch relative to a rolling 48-bar intraday VWAP.
entries.hypothesis: Large positive stretches above rolling VWAP should be prone to short-horizon mean reversion after entry lag and trading costs.

research_memory.negative_findings: Rejected in the 2026-05-15 batch: standalone IC and spread were not stable enough across years, and the feature failed hit-rate/stability gates despite being point-in-time safe.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a different microstructure hypothesis such as volume-weighted mean reversion, signed deviation buckets, or liquidity-conditioned recovery; do not rerun the same raw deviation.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar downside close-return semivolatility using only negative 5-minute returns.
entries.hypothesis: Downside-only realized volatility should be a cleaner long-only risk penalty than symmetric volatility because recent selling pressure worsens both selection quality and execution risk.
entries.notes: Formal 2023-2025 admission cleared all gates in inverted direction. The equal two-factor candidate portfolio was negative and the isolated alpha sleeve lost 34.96%, so do not promote it as standalone alpha. A lagged downside-volatility gross-exposure gate improved the promoted decorrelated policy from 32.21% to 38.77% full-window return, improved high-cost return from 20.31% to 27.31%, reduced drawdown, and passed all standard validation checks.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar skewness of 5-minute close-to-close returns.
entries.hypothesis: Intraday return skewness can separate unstable lottery-like bursts from smoother accumulation; the direction should be learned or gated because positive skew may represent either attention chasing or genuine demand.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 2023-2025 standard admission showed strong inverted rank IC, but the cost-adjusted top-minus-bottom spread was negative, so the factor is not economically admissible as a standalone signal.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a tail-risk filter or after adding liquidity and turnover controls that turn annual cost-adjusted spread positive.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar Chaikin-style money-flow balance from close location within each 5-minute high-low range, weighted by volume.
entries.hypothesis: Closes near intrabar highs on sustained volume should proxy net accumulation more directly than raw volume expansion and may improve attention signals after costs.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 48-bar money-flow factor was rejected in standard admission: coverage was below the hard threshold, directional hit rate was only 0.507, and only one annual slice had stable IC direction.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different accumulation transform, such as volume-decayed close-location value or liquidity-conditioned money flow, and require full coverage plus stable annual direction.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar signed turnover imbalance, using the sign of each 5-minute close-to-close return to classify turnover as up-pressure or down-pressure.
entries.hypothesis: Turnover attached to upticks should be more informative than raw turnover shocks because it combines participation with directional pressure while remaining computable from standard bars.

research_memory.negative_findings: The signed turnover imbalance only worked in inverted direction and failed the cost-adjusted spread gate despite passing basic IC and coverage checks.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only after separating up-pressure and down-pressure legs or adding liquidity participation constraints that demonstrate positive cost-adjusted spread.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar close momentum divided by realized 5-minute return volatility over the same window.
entries.hypothesis: Volatility-scaled intraday continuation may separate durable demand from noisy price jumps, reducing overlap with raw momentum signals that were unstable after costs.

research_memory.negative_findings: Risk-adjusted momentum required inverted treatment, had only two stable annual IC slices, and failed the cost-adjusted spread gate in the standard 2023-2025 admission run.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a reversal/overextension signal with explicit holding-period and liquidity controls, not as raw volatility-scaled continuation.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar close momentum multiplied by current volume expansion versus the same-window rolling mean.
entries.hypothesis: Continuation confirmed by contemporaneous volume expansion should be more robust than raw momentum or raw volume because it requires both price direction and participation.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 2026-05-20 compact-core incremental test did not justify adding this factor to the compact core. Adding it to the four-factor baseline reduced full_base return from 42.92% to 35.58% and full high-cost return from 36.39% to 29.43%. It improved year_2024_base only from -5.96% to -5.18%, worsened January 2024, and left sell-pressure absorption as the largest contribution feature in all 11,033 observed 2024 bars.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Keep as candidate but do not add to the compact-core baseline. Retry only after a sell-pressure contribution cap, health-gated overlay, or execution policy change shows better January/June 2024 robustness without diluting full-window and high-cost returns versus the four-factor compact core.

entries.description: Current 5-minute open-to-previous-close gap.
entries.hypothesis: Short interval gaps capture discrete repricing and temporary order-book imbalance; direction may differ across market regimes, so admission should evaluate both continuation and reversal use.

research_memory.negative_findings: The intraday gap factor was rejected: rank IC was effectively zero, t-stat and hit-rate gates failed, turnover was high, and cost-adjusted spread was negative.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a redesigned event feature with overnight/session boundary separation, limit-event conditioning, or explicit reversal buckets.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar correlation between 5-minute close returns and turnover.
entries.hypothesis: Positive return-turnover correlation indicates participation arriving on up moves rather than indiscriminate turnover spikes, which may be more durable than standalone turnover ratios.

research_memory.negative_findings: Return-turnover correlation worked only in inverted direction, had two stable annual IC slices, and failed the cost-adjusted spread gate in the standard run.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only after decomposing turnover participation into regime or liquidity buckets with positive after-cost spread in annual slices.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar share of 5-minute close-to-close returns that are negative.
entries.hypothesis: A high fraction of recent negative 5-minute returns indicates persistent intraday selling pressure and weak tape quality. Unlike downside volatility or skewness, the transform intentionally ignores return magnitude and turnover so it can test whether loss persistence alone adds a cleaner risk penalty.
entries.notes: Pre-development research-memory check produced only watchlist warnings, chiefly intraday_return_skewness_5m_w48 and intraday_risk_adjusted_momentum_5m_w48. The material difference is that this factor uses only the frequency of negative bars, not return magnitude, skewness, volatility scaling, or turnover participation. As a gross-exposure overlay, it improved the standard promoted policy only when combined with the downside-volatility gate, raising full-window return from 38.77% to 40.73% and high-cost return from 27.31% to 29.19%, but with worse full-window drawdown. A policy drawdown brake evaluated on week streaming chunks improved the combined-gate full-window result to 41.75% return and -6.49% max drawdown, while day chunks were too slow for practical standard validation.

research_memory.negative_findings: The factor passed hard statistical gates with full coverage, strong inverted rank IC, stable direction in 2023-2025, and moderate top-N turnover, but failed the economic gate because inverted top-minus-bottom spread was negative after the 13 bps cost proxy. A lagged standalone risk gate improved the promoted policy versus the ungated baseline but underperformed the existing downside-volatility gate. The combined negative-return-persistence plus downside-volatility gate passed validation and improved full-window and high-cost returns, but worsened drawdown versus the downside-volatility gate alone. A stricter drawdown-guard parameterization passed validation but reduced full-window return to 39.26%, high-cost return to 27.88%, and worsened max drawdown to -8.31%. The existing score-health budget-deadband regime gate passed validation but reproduced the ungated promoted baseline, so it did not replace the factor gate or improve drawdown. A policy-native monthly-chunk drawdown brake interface was added and validated, but the -7% to 0.5x configuration initially reproduced the v1 gate path because the relevant drawdown happens within a streaming chunk. The final rebalance-level fix preserved the -6.49% drawdown improvement with lower operational complexity than day chunking, while day chunks remained too slow for routine validation.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a rebalance-level drawdown brake or liquidity/execution condition. Do not rerun simple negative-return-persistence quantile-threshold tightening, the existing budget-deadband regime schedule, or day-chunk validation, and do not promote as standalone alpha without a positive cost-adjusted spread.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar downside beta against the cross-sectional median market return.
entries.hypothesis: Names that are more exposed to market-wide downside bars should be penalized in a long-only book because they amplify losses during weak tape regimes even when their own volatility is not extreme.
entries.notes: This factor is designed as a cross-sectional downside-risk penalty rather than a reversal transform. It uses the market-wide median 5m return at each timestamp and measures each name's sensitivity only when the market is down, which is materially different from own-return persistence, volatility, or gap response features. The standard single-factor admission rejected it because rank IC strength, t-stat, directional hit rate, and annual stability were all below threshold despite acceptable coverage and turnover.

research_memory.negative_findings: The factor had full coverage and low turnover, but its absolute rank IC, t-stat, directional hit rate, and stable-year count all failed the admission gate. The yearly profile was mixed with 2024 negative and only one stable year, so the market-downside sensitivity signal was not strong enough to survive the standard alpha screen.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different market-state construction, such as sector-relative downside beta or an explicit regime split that conditions on market breadth and limit-up/limit-down pressure.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar resilience of returns during market limit-pressure states.
entries.hypothesis: Names that hold up better when limit-down opens dominate the tape should be more robust long-only holdings than names that simply track the market beta or own volatility.
entries.notes: This factor is materially different from market beta because it conditions on explicit limit-pressure stress using live-open limit flags. It is not an own-path reversal or volatility transform. The first implementation uses a market-wide pressure proxy computed from the same timestamp's limit-up and limit-down open rates. It should be evaluated as a market-state resilience penalty for long-only selection.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 2026-05-20 legacy revalidation rejected the factor because coverage was only about 21.64%, below the 95% hard admission gate. This is a data coverage failure, not a fresh economic-signal rejection.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only after feature coverage is fixed and the shared admission report passes the 95% coverage gate.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar resilience of returns during weak market breadth states.
entries.hypothesis: Names that hold up better when the cross-sectional advance-decline ratio is weak should be more robust long-only holdings than names that rely on broad-market participation.
entries.notes: This factor is intentionally different from the rejected market-downside beta family. It uses cross-sectional advance-decline breadth, not median market return, and therefore satisfies the retry condition that called for a materially different market-state construction based on breadth. Standard single-factor admission selected the inverted direction but rejected the factor because directional IC hit rate and stable-year count failed.

research_memory.negative_findings: The factor had full coverage and passed absolute rank IC, t-stat, cost-adjusted spread, and turnover checks only in the inverted direction, but failed the directional IC hit-rate gate at 49.81% and had only one stable annual IC slice. Yearly rank IC signs were mixed: mildly positive in 2023 and 2024, strongly negative in 2025.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different breadth construction, such as sector-relative breadth, breadth shock persistence, or an explicit non-bull-market regime split that improves 2023 and 2024 without relying on 2025 inversion.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar downside turnover absorption ratio.
entries.hypothesis: Names with unusually high turnover per unit downside return may reflect crowded selling or distribution under pressure; lower downside turnover absorption should be cleaner for long-only selection.
entries.notes: This is not a plain turnover ratio or a market beta proxy. It measures downside-only turnover per unit of cumulative negative return over the same rolling window. The standard admission run found a strong inverted direction, suggesting high turnover during downside bars behaves more like crowded distribution than healthy absorption. Standalone decorrelated portfolio validation was positive in full-window, high-cost, and all annual slices, but 2024 max drawdown remained high enough to keep this as a drawdown-watch candidate rather than an immediate promoted factor. A simple -7% drawdown brake with 0.5x exposure reduced full-window drawdown from -31.84% to -20.76%, but lowered full-window return from 60.79% to 33.70% and made 2024 annual return negative, so it is not the preferred risk-control path. A 2024 quick test using downside-volatility state gating improved the 2024 slice from 4.17% return and -29.66% max drawdown to 11.52% return and -21.53% max drawdown. The same downside-volatility gate failed the standard full-window risk-reward check: full_base return fell to 39.69% while max drawdown only improved to -30.27%, versus the standalone 60.79% and -31.84%. Do not continue this gate configuration without a materially different state variable or exposure rule. The compact-core overlay test found that generic contribution caps diluted full-window and high-cost returns, while health shrink left year_2024_base negative and increased sell-pressure contribution concentration. A sell-pressure-specific mild-slow regime guard improved year_2024_base from -5.96% to -1.96%, but full_base fell from 42.92% to 33.52% and full high-cost fell from 36.39% to 27.88%. A narrower combined-rank v2 guard improved year_2024_base to -3.94% with smaller full-window drag, but missed January 2024 and still left 2024 negative. Do not adopt either guard; move next to complementary factor development or a higher-level regime state.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 2026-05-21 compact-core overlay tests did not justify a generic contribution cap, framework-wide health shrink default, or the first targeted mild-slow sell-pressure regime guard. Contribution cap 0.45 improved year_2024_base from -5.96% to -4.21%, but full_base fell from 42.92% to 34.42% and full high-cost fell from 36.39% to 28.85%. Contribution cap 0.50 reduced full_base to 32.05% and year_2024_base to -6.19%. Health shrink preserved full_base at 42.79% and high-cost at 37.14%, but year_2024_base stayed negative at -5.79% and the average largest 2024 contribution share rose from 0.534 to 0.657. The targeted mild-slow regime guard improved year_2024_base to -1.96% and reduced drawdown, but full_base fell to 33.52%, full high-cost fell to 27.88%, and 2025 fell from 31.85% to 23.08%. The narrower combined-rank v2 guard reduced the full-window cost, but year_2024_base remained negative at -3.94% and January 2024 worsened slightly versus baseline.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Do not continue timestamp-level sell-pressure exposure overlays without a materially different state definition. Prefer complementary factor development or a higher-level day/rebalance regime that explicitly protects January and June 2024 without false positives in September and November.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar decay of downside turnover between earlier and recent half-windows.
entries.hypothesis: Names whose downside turnover has faded across the recent intraday window may reflect seller exhaustion, while names with downside turnover concentrated in the recent half may face intensifying distribution pressure.
entries.notes: This is not a total turnover, signed turnover imbalance, negative-return persistence, sell-pressure absorption, or VWAP-deviation reversal transform. The research-memory check matched the rejected VWAP-deviation factor, but that rejection permits retry under a materially different microstructure hypothesis. This factor satisfies that condition by comparing the earlier and recent halves of the rolling window using only turnover on negative-return bars, so it measures the time profile of downside trading pressure rather than price deviation from a volume-weighted anchor. Standard admission passed in the long direction with full coverage, 0.00688 rank IC, 13.93 t-stat, 53.39% directional hit rate, three stable years, 15.64 bps cost-adjusted spread, and 27.79% top-N turnover. Standard portfolio validation was stopped after full_base evidence because the standalone decorrelated policy returned only 0.73% with -34.29% max drawdown; diagnostics show the long-only top-score basket had negative average forward labels in 2023, 2024, and 2025, so the factor's positive spread came mainly from avoiding even weaker bottom names rather than selecting strong long names.

research_memory.negative_findings: Latest legacy revalidation upgraded single-factor admission to candidate, but high-cost portfolio return remained negative at -1.96%. Treat as cost-fragile and do not promote before a lower-turnover policy fixes the weakness.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with stricter turnover controls, entry/exit buffers, or as a conditional liquidity-state overlay with positive high-cost validation.

entries.description: Slow sell-pressure exhaustion persistence after penalizing short-window relief rallies.
entries.hypothesis: Durable seller exhaustion should be more useful for long-only selection when heavy earlier downside turnover fades, price recovers with upside turnover participation, and the signal is not dominated by short-window relief rallies.
entries.notes: This factor extends the downside-turnover-decay and sell-pressure-recovery research path by requiring price recovery, upside turnover participation, and fading downside turnover, then subtracting short-window exhaustion components to avoid chasing transient relief rallies. Formal admission passed as a long candidate with 0.00812 rank IC, 19.87 t-stat, 56.03% directional hit rate, 7.71 bps cost-adjusted spread, and 3/3 stable years. Standalone primary validation was positive in full-base and high-cost stress, but 2023 and 2024 annual primary returns were negative. The compact-core incremental test reduced contribution concentration and drawdown but cut full-base return from 42.92% to 31.36% and high-cost return from 36.39% to 26.15%, so do not add it to the compact-core default.

research_memory.negative_findings: The factor passed statistical admission and has low correlation to the compact-core factors, but it failed the economic incremental bar. Adding it to the four-factor compact core lowered full-base return by 11.56 percentage points and full high-cost return by 10.24 percentage points. It improved January and June 2024 but worsened March, September, and December, leaving the year_2024_base slice slightly worse than the baseline. Its full-base monitor health score was also the weakest of the five-factor run.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a conditional overlay, a gated entry signal, or under a lower-turnover policy that preserves compact-core full-window and high-cost returns. Do not add another plain sell-pressure exhaustion variant to the compact-core default without showing positive incremental economics.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar price recovery after sell pressure, confirmed by upside turnover participation.
entries.hypothesis: Names that recover more positive return per unit of recent downside return, with recovery confirmed by upside turnover participation, should be better long-only candidates after sell pressure than names that merely avoid the worst downside bucket.
entries.notes: This was explicitly designed as a long-only top-bucket quality signal after the downside-turnover-decay diagnosis. It is not a raw VWAP deviation, reversal transform, sell-pressure absorption ratio, or downside turnover decay. The research-memory check matched the rejected VWAP-deviation factor, but that rejection allows retry with a liquidity-conditioned recovery hypothesis; this factor qualified by using rolling positive-return recovery over downside-return damage and confirming recovery with upside turnover participation. The standard admission run put the factor on watchlist: direction flipped to invert, cost-adjusted spread was -16.56 bps, and the long-only top-bucket health check failed because the highest quantile had negative average forward labels in 2023 and 2024.

research_memory.negative_findings: The recovery transform did not behave as a long-only quality signal. Admission selected the inverted direction, cost-adjusted spread was negative, and the highest quantile had negative average forward labels in 2023 and 2024. Strong apparent sell-pressure recovery likely marks short-horizon exhaustion or overextension rather than a robust next-day long entry.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different recovery definition that separates early rebound from late overextension, or after adding an explicit pullback/entry-price condition. Do not rerun this plain recovery ratio as a standalone long-only factor.

entries.description: Multi-dimensional daily moving-average state broadcast onto the 5-minute research grid using only completed prior trading sessions.
entries.hypothesis: Completed daily moving averages capture slower investor anchoring, trend-following pressure, and crowded-trend dispersion that can complement short-horizon intraday liquidity and volatility factors without using same-day unfinished daily bars.
entries.notes: The implementation deliberately shifts daily moving-average features by one completed trading session before merging back to 5-minute bars. Deviation and spread columns express trend/anchor state; slope columns express trend change; ribbon position, dispersion, and trend score summarize cross-window alignment. The research-memory check matched the rejected intraday_range_position_5m_w48 factor, but this candidate is materially different: it uses completed daily moving-average anchors and cross-window MA ribbon geometry, not a 5-minute rolling high-low range location. Admission must evaluate the generated columns individually before any portfolio-level use. Formal promoted-candidate review on 2026-05-19 accepted the fixed high_dispersion_current ribbon-dispersion gross-exposure gate as the leading promoted challenger while keeping score_budget_gate_v1 as the active primary baseline. Evidence: standard validation improved full return from 14.38% to 20.75%, high-cost return from 9.43% to 16.18%, max drawdown from -19.16% to -16.13%, and turnover from 124.78 to 101.86. Walk-forward fixed challenger sum return was 21.22% versus v1 16.91% with 4/4 positive forward windows, but the dynamic train-window selector was rejected. Promotion checks passed monthly concentration, cost stress, and factor attribution gates. Do not add month-specific patches or switch the default strategy without a separate default-change review.

entries.description: Residual return resilience during weak-breadth and breadth-shock states.
entries.hypothesis: Stocks with positive residual returns during weak-breadth shocks should be more resilient long-only holdings than names that only rise with the broad tape.
entries.notes: Rejected after the 2026-05-21 complementary-factor run. The factor was point-in-time safe and materially different from the older breadth_resilience construction because it used residual stock return versus the cross-sectional median and a lagged breadth-shock component. Formal admission rejected all tested windows. The 48-bar member had rank IC -0.00237, directional hit rate 49.31%, and only one stable annual IC slice; 2025 moved strongly against 2023-2024.

research_memory.negative_findings: All tested breadth-shock residual resilience windows were rejected. The direction selected by admission was inverted, hit rate was below threshold, and yearly IC direction was not stable, especially in 2025.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different regime-conditioned design that proves positive annual direction and cost-aware portfolio contribution; do not rerun plain weak-breadth residual resilience.

entries.description: Rolling log-turnover signal-to-noise ratio used as a liquidity stability proxy.
entries.hypothesis: Stable turnover supply may proxy stable liquidity participation and execution quality, but only if the relationship is monotonic and survives transaction costs.
entries.notes: The 2026-05-21 run showed this ratio is not a clean linear alpha. Admission put all three windows on watchlist with inverted direction and failed cost-adjusted spread. Full-base partial rebalance was positive, but cost-aware optimizer lost roughly 75%-76% across decorrelated, equal, and ic_weighted methods. Treat this as a diagnostic liquidity-state variable, not as a tradable factor.

research_memory.negative_findings: The current turnover-stability ratio is non-linear and cost fragile. It passed hard statistical checks only as a watchlist inverted signal, while cost-aware optimizer full-base validation lost more than 75%.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a non-linear liquidity-state or capacity filter with explicit cost-aware positive validation; do not promote the raw mean/std ratio.

entries.description: Rolling conservative lower bound on log turnover supply.
entries.hypothesis: Low conservative liquidity reliability may proxy a risk premium or small-name elasticity, but it must pass strict capacity and cost-aware checks before use in a long-only A-share strategy.
entries.notes: Formal admission was strong in the inverted direction, with rank IC -0.05998, t-stat -65.08, hit rate 66.01%, stable 3/3 annual IC, and cost-adjusted spread 11.56 bps. The original long-side liquidity-quality hypothesis failed; the admitted effect is a low-reliability risk premium. Quick full-base policy validation kept partial rebalance positive at 46.01%, but cost-aware optimizer returned -33.91% with -43.53% drawdown. Keep on watchlist until a capacity-aware transform or policy proves the premium can be traded after costs.

research_memory.negative_findings: The statistically strong inverted liquidity-reliability premium is not cost robust in quick policy validation. Cost-aware optimizer full-base return was -33.91% with -43.53% max drawdown.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with capacity-aware gating, stricter turnover controls, or a price-confirmed transform that produces positive cost-aware full and annual validation.

entries.description: Low liquidity-reliability premium gated by recent turnover capacity and price recovery.
entries.hypothesis: Low conservative liquidity reliability may be tradable only when recent turnover capacity exists and price has started to recover after sell pressure.
entries.notes: This first capacity-and-recovery transform improved the raw low-liquidity reliability hypothesis statistically, but remained unsuitable for trading. Admission put both windows on watchlist: l48 had rank IC 0.02631, t-stat 33.78, hit rate 60.12%, stable 3/3 annual IC, but cost-adjusted spread -11.05 bps; l96 had rank IC 0.03413, t-stat 40.29, hit rate 61.83%, stable 3/3 annual IC, but cost-adjusted spread -14.66 bps. Quintile evidence was non-monotonic: the middle quantiles were positive while the top bucket was slightly negative, consistent with overextended recovery and crowding risk.

research_memory.negative_findings: The plain monotonic recovery gate produced positive IC but failed the cost-adjusted spread gate and had a non-monotonic quantile shape. Extremely strong recovery after low liquidity reliability behaved like overextension rather than a robust long entry.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different recovery definition that separates moderate recovery from overextended recovery and proves positive cost-aware full and annual validation.

entries.description: Balanced low liquidity-reliability recovery signal that rewards moderate recovery and penalizes overextension.
entries.hypothesis: Low liquidity reliability carries a risk premium only when there is enough recent capacity and the post-sell-pressure recovery is credible but not overextended. Moderate recovery after impaired liquidity can indicate exhausted sellers and tradable elasticity; extreme recovery is more likely crowded rebound or adverse-selection risk.
entries.notes: This factor was developed after the raw low-liquidity reliability and monotonic recovery gates failed cost-aware trading. It uses smooth positive low-reliability scoring, a capacity-balance term peaking near normal recent turnover capacity, and a recovery-balance term peaking near one unit of positive recovery per unit of downside damage. Admission accepted both tested windows as candidates. The l48 member had rank IC 0.05908, t-stat 65.11, hit rate 65.42%, stable 3/3 annual IC, cost-adjusted spread 19.33 bps, and top-N turnover 7.30%. The l96 member had rank IC 0.05618, t-stat 61.08, hit rate 64.44%, stable 3/3 annual IC, cost-adjusted spread 16.26 bps, and top-N turnover 4.30%. Standalone standard policy validation passed with no failed checks: full-base partial return 55.60%, cost-aware return 99.75%, high-cost partial return 48.36%, high-cost cost-aware return 83.36%, and positive primary yearly returns in 2023, 2024, and 2025. Portfolio integration failed the current leading equal annual-budget-52 combination: old leading set plus both balance windows returned only 4.49% with -25.11% max drawdown, and old leading set plus l48 returned 2.80% with -22.85% max drawdown versus the baseline 33.44% with -7.85% max drawdown. Keep on watchlist; do not include in default candidate combinations until a portfolio-level gate or allocator demonstrates incremental value.

entries.description: Lagged same-intraday-slot cross-sectional residual return memory over prior completed sessions.
entries.hypothesis: Some A-share intraday flows may recur at the same time of day, but the tested 5- and 20-session same-slot residual means did not produce a robust or tradable standalone signal.

entries.description: Previous-session close to current-session open gap measured inside the 5-minute intraday feature set.
entries.hypothesis: The session-boundary overnight gap may capture information arrival and opening auction pressure that persists over the next 48 bars when separated from generic within-session 5-minute returns.
entries.notes: Single-factor admission passed in the long direction with full coverage, stable positive annual rank IC across 2023-2025, low top-N turnover, and positive cost-adjusted spread. Research-memory similarity to the rejected raw intraday gap remains a portfolio-review risk, so this needs incremental validation before promotion. Quick portfolio validation passed for the three-feature candidate basket under decorrelated/equal/ic_weighted methods. The primary decorrelated + partial_rebalance_daily result was positive but drawdown-heavy; standard primary-policy validation completed with warn status. Full-base and high-cost decorrelated partial-rebalance returns were positive, but year_2023_base was negative, year_2024_base was only marginally positive, full-window drawdown stayed deep, and January/June 2024 stress losses remained large. Keep as a candidate with drawdown and portfolio-integration watch; do not promote without a drawdown or regime-control test that preserves full-window and high-cost returns. A focused 2024 factor-health shrink experiment failed the high-cost gate: base return fell versus monitor mode, high-cost return turned negative, and max drawdown was effectively unchanged. The gate improved the 2024 annual slice but reduced full-window return versus monitor mode and materially cut the strong 2025 contribution, so reject it as a replacement control for this candidate basket.

entries.description: Weak-market-state interaction of overnight gap-up and gap-response risks.
entries.display_name: Intraday weak-tape overnight gap risk, 48-bar state
entries.hypothesis: The 2023/2024 diagnostics show that the overnight-gap basket can retain positive cross-sectional spread while its top long-only bucket loses money in weak tape. Positive overnight gap, gap-up fade, and gap-down recovery should therefore be penalized when rolling market downside and weak breadth are elevated.
entries.notes: Candidate after the 2026-05-22 weak-tape-gap admission run. Standalone admission accepted two inverted risk legs, but combined evaluation showed the down-recovery leg is a near duplicate of the existing overnight gap-down recovery feature, so the active feature list keeps only the materially incremental gap-up weak-tape risk leg. The gap-up fade leg was excluded because it failed the directional hit-rate and stable-year admission gates and is also a near duplicate of the old gap-up-fade signal. Standalone portfolio validation is positive full-window and high-cost, but remains warning-only because 2023 and 2024 annual slices are negative; generic factor-health shrink did not change the selected path. The deduplicated four-leg basket with the existing 2026-05-22 candidates passed standard validation: full-base return 0.183513, high-cost return 0.130797, and all 2023/2024/2025 annual slices positive. A formal promoted-candidate review accepted the basket for promoted-candidate tracking with no default strategy change. The follow-up existing-artifact frontier comparison confirmed tracking-only: the four-leg basket improves the prior three-leg basket and repairs 2023, but it does not dominate active/default drawdown or the daily-MA frontier full-window and high-cost returns. The 2026-05-23 true unified replay confirmed the four-leg basket as a standalone promoted-tracking candidate with validation status pass, full-base return 0.183513, high-cost return 0.130797, and all annual slices positive. The unified daily-MA frontier plus gap/weak-tape replay improved 2023, 2024, 2025, and worst drawdown versus the daily-MA frontier, but did not dominate full-window or high-cost return and still had a negative 2023 slice, so default strategy change remains false. The controlled score-overlay replay upgraded the factor to a portfolio-construction candidate: a rank-normalized unconditional 5% satellite overlay over the daily-MA frontier improved full-base return to 0.254523, high-cost return to 0.203521, worst drawdown to -0.147765, and full-window turnover to 100.134, but 2023 stayed negative at -0.034703. The 2.5%-7.5% fine grid confirmed 5% as the preferred return/risk tradeoff; 2.5% is the stability fallback with full-base return 0.231244, high-cost return 0.182866, and 2023 return -0.010725. Frozen promotion replay accepted the 5% overlay as the frozen challenger with no default change: it beat the daily-MA frontier on full return by 0.047032, high-cost return by 0.041721, full-window drawdown by 0.025718, and full-window turnover by -1.727207, and monthly concentration checks passed. A later post-2025 unseen replay used the promoted BaoStock CN equity 5m update file through 2026-05-15 after the fast-parquet loader was extended to include update parquet files. That true unseen replay rejects the frozen 5% default-change case: 5% returned -0.007489 full-base and -0.011658 high-cost, while the 2.5% fallback improved primary-only and returned 0.002389 full-base but still -0.001751 high-cost. Follow-up attribution shows the 5% overlay did not fail because top-score label quality collapsed: its average top-minus-universe label was -0.000170 versus -0.003378 for primary-only, but it replaced too much of the primary selection set, with average top-50 overlap only 42.70%-62.77% by month. The main realized weak point was path/state fit in the high-instability 2026 tape, especially March 2026 and reduced/full risk-state bars. A single constrained 2.5% retry that enabled the satellite only in full/reduced risk states was then rejected: full-base return fell to -0.010086 and high-cost return to -0.013968 versus primary-only at -0.002803 and -0.006774. This closes the local overlay-patch path. Default strategy remains unchanged; future work needs a new mechanism or new unseen data rather than another local overlay-weight or state patch. Research-memory check is blocked by prior gap and breadth failures, but the retry conditions explicitly allow a materially different event-state split tied to breadth or market state. This factor is not a plain gap, plain gap-up fade, or standalone breadth resilience feature: it only activates overnight-gap risk under rolling weak-tape state and is intended to repair negative absolute top-bucket returns in 2023/2024.

research_memory.negative_findings: Pre-development memory check was blocked by prior gap-up fade, gap, breadth resilience, and market-downside-beta failures. The rejected gap-up-fade leg confirms that plain fade conditioning remains weak. Combined evaluation also showed near-duplicate exposure: weak-tape down-recovery correlates 0.999776 with overnight gap-down recovery, and weak-tape gap-up fade correlates 0.999996 with overnight gap-up fade.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Only keep the event-state interaction legs that pass admission and are not near-duplicates of existing active candidates. After the 2026 true-unseen replay and the failed full/reduced gated 2.5% retry, do not reopen broad overlay-weight or local state-gate search. Future retries require a new mechanism, new data, or a materially different portfolio allocator.

entries.description: Resilience of stock residual returns during cross-sectional synchronous downside shocks.
entries.hypothesis: Stocks with stronger residual recovery during broad synchronized downside bars should be better long-only candidates after policy or event shocks because selling pressure is absorbed relative to the market tape.
entries.notes: New policy/event shock proxy batch. This feature avoids direct news text and uses only point-in-time OHLCV and price-limit state; cross-sectional market shock state is used as a weight, while the feature value remains stock-specific residual resilience. Research-memory check is blocked by prior event-family rejects, especially plain intraday gap, gap-up fade, and overnight-intraday disagreement. This candidate is materially different because it does not rank raw gaps or disagreement; it ranks rolling stock residual recovery only during synchronized downside pressure.

research_memory.negative_findings: The synchronized-downside residual-resilience proxy failed standard admission: rank IC was only 0.000978, t-stat was 1.04, directional hit rate was 49.75%, and only one annual slice matched the selected long direction.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different synchronized-downside event definition or event subset that improves annual direction and hit rate; do not rerun the same rolling residual-resilience weighting as a standalone factor.

entries.description: Resilience of stock residual returns during limit-up and limit-down diffusion pressure.
entries.hypothesis: During price-limit diffusion after policy or event shocks, the residual-resilience side can identify fragile relief moves; admission selected the inverted side as the stronger long-only ranking.
entries.notes: New policy/event shock proxy batch. Limit diffusion combines the current limit-up/down cross-sectional rates with one-bar increases in those rates, then weights stock-specific residual return. Research-memory check is blocked by prior event-family rejects, especially plain intraday gap, gap-up fade, and overnight-intraday disagreement. This design is materially different because it conditions the cross-section on limit diffusion pressure and ranks residual resilience, not standalone gap or disagreement. Standard admission accepted the inverted direction with -0.011386 rank IC, -15.15 t-stat, 54.70% directional hit rate, two stable years, and 6.81 bps cost-adjusted spread. However, standalone standard portfolio validation failed: full-base return was -0.81%, high-cost return was -5.27%, 2023 and 2024 yearly slices were negative, and full-window max drawdown reached -36.33%. Follow-up controls did not rescue the factor: single-factor health shrink produced the same trading path and failed gates, while the downside-volatility exposure gate worsened full-base return to -9.18% and high-cost return to -14.28%. Event-state diagnostics isolate the toxic states: top-N label was -0.71% in limit_diffusion_extreme and -0.64% in limit_diffusion; 2024-01 combined -16.89% portfolio return with 16.10% limit_diffusion_extreme and 16.76% limit_diffusion timestamp shares. A fixed one-bar-lagged event-state gate that blocks both limit_diffusion and limit_diffusion_extreme improved full-base return to 8.68% and high-cost return to 2.54%, with 2024 slightly positive and 2025 still positive, but 2023 remained -9.78% and max drawdown stayed deep. Stacking a fixed -7%/0.5x drawdown brake on top of the same lagged block reduced full-window max drawdown to -21.45% and turnover to 56.87, but over-delevered after losses: full-base return was -7.99%, high-cost return was -7.81%, and all annual slices were negative. A fixed complementary selection blend using already governed sell-pressure absorption, 6-bar volatility, Amihud, and 48-bar efficiency candidates with the same lagged event-state block produced a strong monitor-only follow-up: full-base return 43.06%, high-cost return 34.99%, 2023 return 8.89%, and 2025 return 30.96%, but remained warning-level because 2024 was -2.89% and max drawdown was -31.95%. Residual-risk diagnostics showed the remaining losses were linked more to selected-basket entry-quality deterioration than to one-off feature concentration. Enabling the already defined lagged factor-health shrink on the same complementary blend and event-state block passed standard validation: full-base return 33.81%, high-cost return 26.27%, 2023 return 5.64%, 2024 return 2.45%, 2025 return 36.18%, and full-window max drawdown improved to -29.79%. Keep the raw event factor on watchlist and do not use it as a standalone leg; the validated construction is the complementary blend with lagged limit-diffusion state block and lagged factor-health shrink. Independent robustness follow-up kept the same fixed blend, lagged limit-diffusion state block, and lagged factor-health shrink. The robust profile passed with zero failures and warnings: zero-cost return was 69.19%, base-cost return was 33.81%, and high-cost return was 26.27%. Same-bar bar-participation capacity diagnostics at 5% and 2% stayed positive with near-unchanged drawdown (33.76% and 34.37% returns), so the research-size path is not fragile to these first-pass caps. Fixed rebalance-cadence checks reject faster 24-bar trading because it returned -1.30%, drew down -34.66%, and raised turnover to 305.43. The 96-bar sensitivity returned 42.51% with lower turnover, but max drawdown worsened to -31.69%; keep 48 bars as the governed default pending future out-of-sample confirmation.

research_memory.negative_findings: Single-factor admission passed in the inverted direction, but standard standalone portfolio validation failed: full-base return was -0.81%, high-cost return was -5.27%, 2023 returned -10.55%, 2024 returned -8.98%, and full-window max drawdown reached -36.33%. Single-factor health shrink lowered the single-leg score scale but did not change the trading path, so full-base and high-cost returns stayed negative. The downside-volatility exposure gate changed the path but worsened economics: full-base return fell to -9.18%, high-cost return fell to -14.28%, 2023 stayed negative at -13.66%, and turnover rose to 159.52. Timestamp event-state diagnostics show that selected top-N labels are most negative in limit_diffusion_extreme (-0.71%) and limit_diffusion (-0.64%) states; 2024-01 was the worst month at -16.89% portfolio return with negative score rank IC and elevated limit-diffusion state shares. A fixed one-bar-lagged full block of limit_diffusion and limit_diffusion_extreme states cleared full-base (+8.68%) and high-cost (+2.54%) return checks, but remained only a warning-level result because 2023 stayed negative at -9.78% and full/high-cost drawdowns stayed around -34% to -35%. A fixed drawdown brake stacked on that block improved max drawdown to -21.45% and reduced turnover to 56.87, but failed economics: full-base return was -7.99%, high-cost return was -7.81%, and 2023, 2024, and 2025 were all negative. A fixed complementary selection blend with governed liquidity/risk/efficiency candidates cleared full/high-cost checks in monitor-only mode, but still had residual yearly instability: 2024 remained negative at -2.89% and full-window max drawdown was -31.95%. Independent robustness checks confirm the governed construction but also bound its use: faster 24-bar rebalancing failed after costs with -1.30% return, -34.66% max drawdown, and 305.43 turnover, while the 96-bar sensitivity had higher return but deeper drawdown than the 48-bar baseline.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Do not retry the raw inverted limit-diffusion score as a standalone candidate portfolio leg. Do not continue the current downside-volatility gross-exposure gate, path-level drawdown-brake threshold search, manual weight retuning, 24-bar faster-rebalance path, or tuning against specific months or years. The validated continuation path is the fixed complementary blend with the lagged limit_diffusion and limit_diffusion_extreme event-state block plus lagged factor-health shrink using the 48-bar rebalance baseline. Robust/zero-cost, high-cost, 5%/2% bar-participation capacity, fixed 24/96-bar cadence sensitivities, allocator packaging, capacity monitoring, and daily paper-monitoring entry points have been logged. This round is complete; future work should use out-of-sample paper/live-simulation monitoring and a separate promotion review on newly arriving data.

entries.description: Price recovery quality weighted by turnover dislocation from the stock's prior intraday turnover baseline.
entries.hypothesis: Event shocks often create volume cliffs or abnormal participation. Admission found the inverted turnover-dislocation recovery ranking statistically stronger, but the standalone economic spread is not yet cost-safe.
entries.notes: New policy/event shock proxy batch. The dislocation z-score is computed against a lagged 48-bar log-turnover baseline, so the effective warmup is roughly 96 bars. Research-memory check is blocked by prior event-family rejects, especially plain intraday gap, gap-up fade, and overnight-intraday disagreement. This candidate is materially different because the event intensity comes from abnormal turnover relative to a lagged baseline and ranks recovery during dislocated participation. Standard admission put the factor on watchlist in the inverted direction: rank IC was -0.016964 with -23.06 t-stat and three stable years, but cost-adjusted spread was -13.70 bps.

research_memory.negative_findings: The turnover-dislocation recovery proxy had strong inverted IC and three stable years, but the selected direction produced negative cost-adjusted spread under the standard 13 bps round-trip cost proxy.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a targeted risk overlay or with an execution-aware transform that makes the directional top-minus-bottom spread positive after costs; do not promote the raw rolling turnover-dislocation recovery score.

entries.description: Current-bar recovery from open weighted by open jump magnitude versus prior close.

entries.hypothesis: After open jumps caused by policy or event repricing, names that recover better from the open relative to the jump magnitude should have stronger short-horizon demand quality.
entries.notes: New policy/event shock proxy batch. This is not the prior overnight-gap tug-of-war feature: it uses bar-level open-to-prior-close jump magnitude as event intensity and averages open-to-close recovery quality over the rolling window. Research-memory check is blocked by prior event-family rejects, especially plain intraday gap, gap-up fade, and overnight-intraday disagreement. This candidate is materially different because it ranks jump-weighted recovery quality over repeated observable jumps rather than the raw jump or one overnight interaction. Standard admission rejected the factor: the selected direction was inverted, but rank IC was only -0.000822 with -1.54 t-stat and negative cost-adjusted spread.

research_memory.negative_findings: The open-jump recovery-quality proxy failed standard admission. The inverted direction had weak rank IC, insignificant t-stat, and negative cost-adjusted spread despite acceptable coverage and two stable annual slices.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different jump-state definition, such as auction-volume or limit-pressure conditioning, that clears IC strength and after-cost spread gates; do not rerun the same open-jump-weighted recovery score.

entries.description: Rolling Herfindahl-like concentration of volume inside the window.
entries.hypothesis: Low rolling volume concentration may proxy steadier participation and more reliable near-term returns than concentrated liquidity events.
entries.notes: 2026-05-25 standalone admission passed, but isolated candidate portfolio, full-basket compact-core overlay, focused concentration blend, focused downside-penalty overlay, and hard entry-exclusion validations were negative. The constrained cost-aware optimizer risk-penalty overlay is promising under a path gross-turnover budget of 155: w25 improves full-base return from 5.92% to 7.13% and w50 improves high-cost and drawdown metrics versus control. A static budget100 cost-robustness follow-up turns high-cost return positive for w25/w50 (w50 high-cost +0.93%, full-base +7.22%) but fails annual stability with 2024 base return of -12.35% for w50 and -12.71% for w25. A budget155 net-edge/pacing probe ruled out small constant net-edge thresholds and uniform path-budget pacing. The first state-aware policy probe found a useful 2024-stability lead but no high-cost fix: top150 risk q90 scale 0.50 improves 2024 to +5.07% while leaving high-cost at -3.08%; drawdown brake fails badly with high-cost -11.03% and 2024 -5.04%. Keep on watchlist, not candidate; next retry should target direct cost-pressure or realized-spend controls.

research_memory.negative_findings: The volume-concentration family is not promoted as a standalone long-only alpha: the isolated 7-feature time-series-decomposition portfolio was negative, and same-policy rank blending, downside score penalties, and hard entry exclusion all underperformed the compact-core control. Static budget100 fixed high-cost stress but failed 2024 annual stability, while small net-edge thresholds, uniform pacing, and drawdown brakes were rejected. The successful branch is portfolio-native only: using the concentration satellite as an optimizer risk penalty with a 155 path turnover budget plus realized cost-pressure turnover cap at 1000 bps / 0.01 per rebalance passed standard validation. w50 returned 16.07% full-base, 8.10% high-cost, kept all annual base slices positive, and improved full-base return versus the no-penalty cost-pressure control.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Use this family only through the validated optimizer-risk-penalty and cost-pressure turnover-cap integration. Do not retry direct rank blending, downside-penalty overlays, hard entry-exclusion thresholds, another static turnover-budget grid, small constant net-edge thresholds, uniform path-budget pacing, or drawdown brakes without a materially new allocator or risk objective.
